<!-- loading: on-demand (invoked via the Skill tool when needed) — generated by scripts/list-skills.sh --annotate; do not hand-edit -->

# Google Play submission requirements

> **Project-scoped.** Loaded on-demand by **planning** (to emit submission acceptance
> criteria into `acceptance.md` early) and **deployment** (to make the existing Gradle /
> Play Console recipe's requirements accountable — `docs/pipeline-deployment-targets.md`).
> The Android sibling of `app-store-submission-requirements`. Most rejections/blocks come
> from things cheap to get right up front and expensive at upload. Planning's job is to turn
> each applicable item below into an **acceptance criterion** so it rides `criteria_covered`
> and is verified before the PR. **Reduced-assurance note:** Android has no gate adapters yet
> (`docs/ios-swiftui-target-plan.md` has no Android sibling), so a run is stamped
> `reduced (android adapters absent)` — this skill makes submission requirements accountable
> at the *planning/AC* level even while the deterministic gates are Python/JS-shaped.

## Policy floors that BLOCK the upload (deterministic — `store-compliance.sh` also checks these)

- **`targetSdkVersion` floor** ⚠ — Google raises the required target API level **annually**;
  a new or updated app below the current floor is **blocked at upload**. Set `targetSdk` to
  the current required level (look it up at build time — it is policy-pinned, not code-pinned).
  Avoid hiding it behind an unresolved Gradle variable/version-catalog the check can't read.
- **Release build hygiene** ⚠ — `android:debuggable="false"` (never `true`) and no
  `usesCleartextTraffic="true"` in the release manifest; strip debug logging/test endpoints.
- **App signing** — enroll in **Play App Signing**; upload key kept as a CI secret (already in
  the Gradle Play Publisher recipe, `pipeline-deployment-targets.md`).

## Data safety & privacy — the highest-frequency review area

- **Data safety form** (Play Console) ⚠ — you must declare every data type the app
  **collects/shares**, its purpose, and whether it's encrypted in transit + user-deletable.
  It must **match actual behavior** (and any bundled SDK's collection) — a mismatch is a
  rejection/removal risk. This is the Play counterpart of Apple's privacy nutrition label;
  reconcile it against the DP workstream's classified-field inventory (`data_surface`).
- **Privacy policy URL** ⚠ — required whenever you collect personal/sensitive data (and always
  for apps targeting children).
- **Permissions** ⚠ — declare only what you use; each `<uses-permission>` must map to real
  functionality. **High-risk/sensitive permissions** (location background, SMS/Call Log, "All
  files access" `MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE`, package visibility) need a **declaration form** and
  a documented core-feature justification, or the app is rejected. Prefer scoped alternatives.
- **Foreground-service types** — declare the correct `foregroundServiceType` and justify it;
  undeclared/misused foreground services are a common block.

## Accounts, auth, payments (frequent rejections)

- **In-app account deletion** ⚠ — apps offering account creation **must** provide in-app
  account deletion **and a web-accessible deletion URL** (Play requires the external path too —
  this is stricter than Apple's in-app-only rule). Make both acceptance criteria when auth exists.
- **Play Billing** ⚠ — digital goods/subscriptions must use **Google Play Billing**, not an
  external processor (physical goods/services use other methods). Wrong billing = hard rejection.
  Flag the monetization model in planning.
- **Login not required to browse** — don't gate non-account content behind a mandatory login.

## Content & metadata (checked in review)

- Store listing complete: title/short/full description, **screenshots + feature graphic**,
  content rating (IARC questionnaire), category, contact + privacy-policy URLs.
- **Target audience & content** (Families policy if it may appeal to children — extra rules).
- No placeholder content, broken links, or deceptive behavior; provide test/demo credentials
  when login is required for review.
- Sufficient functionality/value — a thin WebView wrapper risks a "minimum functionality"-class
  rejection (Play's analogue of Apple 4.2).

## Common rejection/blocking causes (design them out early)

- `targetSdk` below the floor (hard upload block). ⚠
- Data safety form missing or mismatched with actual collection. ⚠
- A sensitive/high-risk permission with no declaration form or core-feature justification. ⚠
- Missing in-app **or** web account-deletion path. ⚠
- Non-Play-Billing payment for digital goods.
- Debuggable/cleartext release build; crash on cold start (review runs the app).

## Acceptance criteria planning must emit (when applicable)

Translate each applicable item into an `acceptance.md` criterion so it's verified pre-PR, e.g.:

- `AC | targetSdk meets Google Play's current floor | build.gradle | store-compliance.sh + build check`
- `AC | Data safety declaration matches the code's collected data types | Play Console + data map | reconcile vs data_surface (DP)`
- `AC | every <uses-permission> maps to a used feature; sensitive ones have a declaration + justification | manifest | grep permission-gated APIs ↔ manifest`
- `AC | authenticated app offers in-app AND web account deletion | Settings flow + web URL | UI test + link check`
- `AC | digital purchases use Google Play Billing | purchase flow | code + manual` (if monetized)
- `AC | release build: debuggable=false, no cleartext, debug logging stripped | release manifest/config | store-compliance.sh + archive inspection`

Planning marks non-applicable items N/A with a one-line reason; it does not invent requirements
the app doesn't trigger. Deployment confirms signing/metadata against the Gradle Play Publisher
recipe before submission. **Both-stores note:** when a project targets Apple *and* Google Play,
emit each store's ACs and reconcile the shared ones (account deletion, data declaration, billing)
once against both policies — Play's web-deletion-path and Data safety requirements are the stricter side.
